title: Centrolepis exserta

The family Restionaceae is a group of monocotyledonous plants, commonly known as restiads or rushes. Restionaceae is further classified under the order Poales and class Liliopsida. This classification places Centrolepis exserta within the broader group of flowering plants.

intro: Centrolepis exserta, also known as the New Zealand thread fern or Centrolepis, is a small, perennial plant species belonging to the family Centrolepidaceae. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. Bioactive constituents of Centrolepis exserta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
